purpose: Controlled evidence ingestion for Phase 7 governance loop validation. NOT proving semantic truth — exercising evidence reconciliation against existing CEU state.

evidence_sources.description: NetBox application registry documentation — reveals extras.registry as central infrastructure component
evidence_sources.structural_relevance: HIGH — directly reveals cross-cutting dependency architecture

evidence_sources.description: Tenancy feature documentation — reveals tenancy assignment reaches across all domain apps
evidence_sources.structural_relevance: HIGH — confirms hero moment about tenancy hidden behavioral authority

evidence_sources.description: NetBox models documentation — feature matrix, model index with per-app model counts
evidence_sources.structural_relevance: HIGH — quantifies model distribution and cross-cutting feature mixin patterns

evidence_sources.description: NetBox official repository architecture map — canonical app descriptions, dependency documentation
evidence_sources.structural_relevance: HIGH — authoritative external documentation of intended architecture

evidence_attachments.description: Application registry lives in extras.registry. The registry is described as 'application-wide parameters' that 'cannot be deleted or overwritten.' Stores include: counter_fields, data_backends, denormalized_fields, filtersets, model_features, models, plugins, request_processors, search, tables, views. Every other app implicitly depends on extras for cross-cutting features. CEU-EXTRAS is classified CONSUMER but evidence suggests FOUNDATION or INFRASTRUCTURE.

AGENTS.md explicitly describes extras as 'Cross-cutting features (custom fields, tags, webhooks, scripts)' and states 'New cross-cutting features belong here.' This is infrastructure language, not consumer language.

Feature matrix in models.md lists 14 cross-cutting model features, all registered through extras. BookmarksMixin, ChangeLoggingMixin, CloningMixin, ContactsMixin, CustomFieldsMixin, CustomLinksMixin, etc. Every NetBoxModel inherits these from extras infrastructure.

Tenancy feature docs list 30+ model types across ALL domain apps that support tenant assignment: Circuits, Cables, Devices, VDCs, Power Feeds, Racks, Sites, Locations, ASNs, Prefixes, IP ranges, IP addresses, VLANs, VRFs, Route targets, Clusters, VMs, L2VPNs, Tunnels, WLANs, WLinks. Confirms hero moment: tenancy is documented as 'optional organizational metadata' but structurally provides behavioral framework for all domains.

Models index shows tenancy provides: Contact, Tenant (Primary), ContactRole (Organizational), ContactGroup, TenantGroup (Nested Group). Despite small model count (5 primary/org models), tenancy's inheritance authority (226 cross-domain edges) vastly exceeds its import authority — confirmed by dual structural authority hero moment.

Models index confirms DCIM dominates with ~30+ primary models (Device, Cable, CableBundle, DeviceType, Module, ModuleType, PowerFeed, PowerPanel, Rack, RackReservation, RackType, Site, VirtualChassis, VDC) plus 10 component models, 10 component template models. Largest app by model count — aligns with CENTRALITY hero moment (dcim/models/__init__.py at 160 inbound imports).

AGENTS.md explicitly describes utilities as 'Shared utilities (no models)'. Confirms FOUNDATION tier classification and INHERITANCE_DOMINANT authority pattern — utilities provides shared infrastructure without own model authority.

AGENTS.md describes netbox/ as 'Core settings, URLs, WSGI, plugin infrastructure' with sub-components: api/, graphql/, models/, navigation/, plugins/, registry.py, search/, ui/. Confirms FOUNDATION tier classification — netbox is the framework itself.

challenged.challenge_basis: Three independent evidence sources (application registry docs, AGENTS.md, feature matrix) all describe extras as cross-cutting infrastructure that every other app depends on. CONSUMER tier implies it consumes services; evidence shows it PROVIDES cross-cutting infrastructure.

confirmed_with_refinement.refinement: Authority pattern fully confirmed by external docs. Tenancy reaches across ALL domain apps via tenant assignment. Dual authority (import vs inheritance) hero moment confirmed by docs. Current OPERATIONAL_DOMAIN tier is correct but authority_pattern should be recorded as DUAL_AUTHORITY_CROSS_CUTTING.

confirmed.finding: Model dominance confirmed by model index
